rulings.Case Title: KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. JOHN ARTHUR SEBASTINELLI et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, FEBRUARY 25, 2008 LINE 3. CROSS-DEFENDANT KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES' DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. KEARNEY STREET IS NOT NAMED. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. JOHN ARTHUR SEBASTINELLI et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, FEBRUARY 25, 2008 LINE 3. CROSS-DEFENDANTS RAUL AGUILAR AND AGUILAR LAW OFFICES' DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COURT IS UNAWARE OF ANY FACTS THAT WOULD GIVE RISE TO A CLAIM BETWEEN THESE PARTIES FOR INDEMNITY OR CONTRIBUTION. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. JOHN ARTHUR SEBASTINELLI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Determining Whether Payment Of Attorney'S Fees Is Subject To A Lien Filed Dec. 5, 2007
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, FEBRUARY 25, 2008 LINE 3. DEFENDANTS JOHN SEBASTINELLI, JOHN SEBASTINELLI, PATRICIA SEBASTINELLI, JOHN SEBASTINELLI, AND MICHELMAN & ROBINSON, L.L.P.'S MOTION For An Order Determining Whether Payment Of Attorney'S Fees Is Subject To A Lien Filed Dec. 5, 2007: FEES ARE TO BE PAID TO KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES. KENT'S LIEN APPLIES TO "JUDGMENT PROCURED"; AN AWARD OF FEES AS A SANCTION IS NOT A JUDGMENT. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: MARINA GOUCHANSKAIA VS. BLOOMINGDALE'S et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, FEBRUARY 25, 2008 LINE 5. DEFENDANT BLOOMINGDALE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE OF A DISCRIMINATORY MOTIVE IS PROBLEMATIC. ASSUMING PLAINTIFF HAS MET HER BURDEN, DEFENDANT HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF A LEGITIMATE REASON FOR NOT HIRING PLAINTIFF, HER COMBATIVE CONDUCT AS DESCRIBED BY GASSENSMITH, SVENSEN AND MASON. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE THAT SHE WAS NOT HIRED FOR REASONS OF NATIONAL ORIGIN IS BASED ON A SINGLE REMARK. AS A MATTER OF LAW, THAT REMARK FALLS WITH THE STRAY REMARK DOCTRINE DESCRIBED IN MARQUES V BANK OF AMERICA (N.D. CAL. 1999) 59 F. SUPP 2D 1005, 1019-1020. DEFENDANT'S PROPOSED ORDER GRANTING THE MOTION REFLECTS THE COURT'S REASONS FOR GRANTING THIS MOTION. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: KAREN BREITENSTEIN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, FEBRUARY 25, 2008 LINE 9. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR A DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY.
